data.title: EGG Media confirms Sports.com to acquire Racing Women assets in $1M deal
data.description:
• EGG Media (NASDAQ:SEGG) on Wednesday confirmed that Sports.com Media Group has signed a letter of intent to invest in women's sports by acquiring all the assets of Racing Women.
  • Under the LOI, Sports.com Media will become the rights holder to Racing Women and acquire a 51% controlling stake in a newly created entity that holds all the assets of Racing Women at a $1 million valuation, with options to expand to full ownership.
  • The acquisition includes Racing Women’s intellectual property and global rights, establishing the initiative as a core asset within SEGG Media’s motorsport expansion strategy.
  • EGG Media (NASDAQ:SEGG) shares rose nearly 5% in premarket trading.

data.title: WNS launches new AI platform for transforming finance organizations
data.description:
• WNS Limited (NYSE:WNS) announced  on Wednesday the launch of Agile Target Operating Model (aTOM), a proprietary AI-led digital platform for driving end-to-end transformation of global finance organizations. 
  • The platform delivers transformation across the entire F&A value chain, including Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, General Ledger, FP&A, and Tax & Treasury.
  • Early collaborations with clients using aTOM have resulted in cost savings of approximately 40%, productivity enhancements of more than 50%, improved working capital, and increased AI adoption, the company said. 
  • WNS also plans to leverage the aTOM platform into other organizational functional areas beyond finance.

data.title: Nuvini Group launches NuviniAI index
data.description:
• Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) announced on Wednesday the launch of the NuviniAI Index, a showcase, and performance tracker of AI-driven tools designed to assess the level of AI adoption in organizations through an objective questionnaire. 
  • The initiative aims to drive internal portfolio transformation and enhance external M&A qualification.
  • For current portfolio companies, the NuviniAI Index serves as a management and transformation framework. 
  • Each company is scored and benchmarked against industry peers and the broader NuviniAI ecosystem, creating an objective baseline for investment decisions. 
  • Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) shares were up more than 3% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Rezolve AI secures $200M oversubscribed financing
data.description:
• Rezolve AI (NASDAQ:RZLV) announced it has signed agreements with two existing institutional investors for an oversubscribed $200 million private placement (before fees and expenses).
  • The deal is expected to close around September 25, 2025, pending customary conditions.
  • The company plans to use the funds to invest for accelerated investment into its sales organization, pursue potential M&A, and support working capital and general corporate needs.
  • RZLV +1.67% premarket to $7.3
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CAVA expands in Michigan with first downtown Detroit restaurant
data.description:
• CAVA (NYSE:CAVA) said on Wednesday that it will open its second location in Michigan, and first in the Detroit metro area, on Friday, September 26, located at 636 Woodward Ave, Detroit, MI 48226. 
  • CAVA Detroit - Woodward Avenue is a 3,000-square-foot location with an indoor dining room and digital order pick-up and delivery.
  • CAVA’s new Detroit restaurant will employ 25-40 local people.

data.title: Integral Ad Science to go private in $10.30/share all-cash deal
data.description:
• Integral Ad Science (NASDAQ:IAS) shares surged nearly 20% premarket on Wednesday after the company entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by private equity firm Novacap in an all-cash transaction.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Novacap will acquire all outstanding shares of IAS for $10.30 per share in cash. This represents a premium of around 22% to IAS's closing share price on Sept. 23, 2025.
  • The transaction values IAS at ~$1.9 billion and positions it for further growth as the company continues investing in AI-first technology.
  • The deal is expected to close before the end of 2025, and, upon completion, IAS will become a privately held company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Acadia slumps after trial setback for Prader-Willi syndrome drug; Soleno jumps
data.description:
Acadia Pharma (NASDAQ:ACAD) shares fell in the premarket on Wednesday after the biotech announced that its experimental therapy for a rare genetic condition known as Prader-Willi syndrome failed in a Phase 3 trial, boosting the shares of its rival drug developer, Soleno Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SLNO).

San Diego, California-based Acadia (NASDAQ:ACAD) lost ~13%, while Soleno (NASDAQ:SLNO), whose PWS candidate is currently in late-stage development, added ~14%.

Citing topline data from its Phase 3 COMPASS PWS trial, the company said that its intranasal PWS therapy, carbetocin, designed to treat the excessive sense of hunger seen with the disease, didn’t reach the study’s main goal with a statistically significant effect.

The effects in those who received carbetocin failed to show any differentiation from patients treated with placebo on any secondary endpoint, Acadia (NASDAQ:ACAD) added, noting that the safety and tolerability of the drug were consistent with its prior clinical trials.

Given the setback, the company has decided to drop intranasal carbetocin from its pipeline.

data.title: MAIA Biotechnology awarded $2.3 million grant by NIH
data.description:
• MAIA Biotechnology (NYSE:MAIA) on Wednesday said that the National Institutes of Health [NIH] has awarded a $2.3 million grant for the expansion of its THIO-101 Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating ateganosine as a third-line treatment for patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer [NSCLC]. 
  • The grant is intended to support expenses related to the enrollment of U.S. patients who are resistant to chemo and immunotherapy. The NIH grant allocations will be distributed over three years from 2025-2027.
  • MAIA +3.26% premarket to $1.58.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FAT Brands taps advisors for $1.2B debt restructuring - report
data.description:
FAT Brands (NASDAQ:FAT) is reportedly working with creditors to restructure its $1.2B in debt, according to sources cited by Bloomberg, working privately with advisors while its creditors take advice from investment bank Houlihan Lokey.  

The discussions are in an effort to restructure FAT Brands (NASDAQ:FAT) debt with which the company has struggled to service as the parent company of Johnny Rockets, Fatburger, and Twin Peaks continues to operate at a loss.

One of the options available for FAT Brands is a whole business securitization in which a company sells all or a significant portion of its cash-flowing assets to a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV). The SPV then sells bonds which are serviced by the cash flow generated by the underlying assets. The structure allows companies to borrow at lower rates because the securitized assets are separated from the parent company.

This method of debt restructuring has previously been used by Wendy’s (WEN), Domino’s Pizza (DPZ), and Planet Fitness (PLNT), among others.

A filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday disclosed the company’s plans to sell more shares in its Twin Peaks business.

 FAT Brands (NASDAQ:FAT) shares closed nearly 13% higher on Tuesday.

data.title: Nasdaq to delist certain securities suspended from trading
data.description:
Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) on Wednesday announced the delisting of certain securities that were previously suspended from trading on its platform.

Listed below are the securities:

  • Digital Brands Group (DBGI) warrants - The warrants were suspended on December 18, 2024
  • Lipella Pharmaceuticals (OTC:LIPO) common stock - The stock was suspended on June 20, 2025
  • ZyVersa Therapeutics (OTC:ZVSA) common stock - The stock was suspended on July 17, 2025
  • Crown LNG (OTC:CGBSF) ordinary shares and warrants - The securities were suspended on July 17, 2025
  • Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics (OTC:BCLI) common stock - The stock was suspended on July 18, 2025
  • Wag! Group (OTC:PETXQ) common stock and warrants - The securities were suspended on July 30, 2025
  • Ontrak (OTC:OTRKQ) common stock - The stock was suspended on August 18, 2025
  • TPI Composites (OTC:TPICQ) common stock - The stock was suspended on August 19, 2025
  • Embrace Change Acquisition (OTC:EMCGF) ordinary shares, units, warrants, and rights - The securities were suspended on August 21, 2025
  • ModivCare (OTC:MODVQ) common stock - The stock was suspended on August 28, 2025
